text.title: graph-rag-production Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for graph-rag-production as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Building a production Graph RAG system on top of NetworkX without a persistence layer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: NetworkX graphs are in-memory only. A process restart loses the entire graph and forces a full re-index. Any production deployment needs a persistent graph store (Neo4j, Kuzu, FalkorDB) so the graph survives restarts and can be accessed by multiple query processes.

failure-mode.description: Running entity extraction without entity normalization before graph insert.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM extraction produces variant spellings of the same entity across chunks ("OpenAI", "openai", "Open AI"). Without normalization (lowercase, strip punctuation, resolve aliases), each variant becomes a separate node in the graph and relationship paths between them are broken.
